name: Saurian Indwelling and Mimesis
desc: A Natlanese mechanism for both exploration and combat that allows the player to take the form of Saurians and their abilities.

content.text: Saurian Indwelling is a game mechanism introduced in
or adult Saurians found in the wild, players can Indwell into that specific Saurian and assume their abilities. During the
, Normal Attack, Elemental Skill and Elemental Burst are replaced with the Saurian's abilities.
This mechanism is not region specific thus any character can be used for Indwelling.
Players can Indwell into 6 types of Saurians introduced in
Tepetilisaurs are the tribal companions of
They have the ability to drill into the ground and move freely, climb
and are highly sensitive to buried treasures.
Koholasaurs are the tribal companions of
They have the ability to swim on water,
Yumkasaurs are the tribal companions of
They have the ability to use grappling hooks to move in air freely, grab items and use
Qucusaurs are the tribal companions of
They have the ability to glide through the air for long periods, and ascend with
Iktomisaurs are the tribal companions of
They have the ability to perform high leaps, enter "Spiritspeaker" state, and scan
Tatankasaurs are the tribal companions of
They have the ability to perform a Quick Charge, and enter the Spirited state to push heavy objects, break through
, make flying leaps using terrain and even briefly dash across water or liquid Phlogiston.
There are two ways to Indwell to a Saurian:
. It will also spawn a Spiritsconce when a wild adult Saurian is defeated in battle. After it is used for Indwelling, it will respawn in 15 seconds.
Wild adult Saurians can be found in the wild or certain quest domains. After defeat in battle, it will respawn alive after 1 minute.
The Indwelling activation has a cooldown of 4 seconds and unlimited duration. However, when Phlogiston runs out, the Saurian will be unable to perform its abilities until it is recovered.
Each Saurian has its own HP bar during the
. This HP can be lost during battle or from Fall Damage. When the Saurian is defeated in battle or other natural means such as drowning, the player will be ejected.
Reaching a Statue of the Seven or a Teleport Waypoint

By holding Elemental Burst, when the player tries to leave Natlan or after the Saurian is defeated, the player will be ejected from the Indwelt State. During a manual ejection, it will leave a Spiritsconce specific to the Indwelt Saurian on the ground.

During the Indwelt State, players will be able to interact with Saurians and activate special dialogues, but it is not possible to talk to humans. Interacting with Statues of the Seven, Tablet of Tona, or cooking is also not possible.
Phlogiston is a special high-temperature energy that can be found throughout Natlan. The energy is necessary to be able to use Saurian abilities.
During the Indwelt State, there will be a Phlogiston bar unique to the Saurian and it will be prioritized over the player's reserves.
Reaching a Statue of the Seven or a Teleport Waypoint
Collecting Candlecap Mushrooms, Crystal Beetles or Pyro Crystalflies
Destroying Phlogiston Piles and Phlogiston Explosive Barrels
, the player's abilities will be switched with the specific Saurian's abilities.

: Create a shockwave on the ground. It will deal

: Perform a long distance high-speed leap forward.
: Perform a kick. It will deal

Normal Attack/Jump, while in air or falling down
in any direction. The player can also move freely for a few seconds before the ability is canceled and goes into cooldown.
Elemental Skill, after first use and while in air
: The Elemental Skill is modified and using it will consume Phlogiston. The Elemental Skill will then be made unavailable until it lands on the ground.
: Hover in the air, change the direction and leap forward.
: When facing them, the Elemental Skill is modified. Use it to grapple and leap to the Coilgrass Sigil.

: When facing them, the Elemental Skill is modified. Use it to grab them. The Elemental Skill will be modified again, allowing to hurl them in any direction and cause explosions. This action will continuously consume Phlogiston until they're hurled.
: Attacks with its claws forward, dealing

: Performs a small jump. When a certain distance away from the ground or by a cliff, Qucusaurus takes to the air and glide. When altitude is insufficient, it will take flight after tapping Jump again and consuming Phlogiston.
It is a special state for Qucusaurus and some of its abilities will change.
: Tap to perform a rolling dash, dealing
. Hold to launch a Plunging Attack, which can be canceled mid-duration by releasing, returning to the gliding state. If the Plunging Attack lands, it can cause a Phlogiston Current.
: Make the Saurus slowly ascend consuming Phlogiston.
: Consume Phlogiston to accelerate Qucusaurus glide to any direction.

: Qucusaurs can take ascend in Gaseous Phlogiston without getting burned. They can also pick up some Gaseous Phlogiston Lumps. Using their power, or ascending in Gaseous Phlogiston Qucusaurs can maintain a state of having full Phlogiston and not consuming any. Using a Plunging Attack while in this state can even result in the creation of a short-lived Phlogiston Current.
: Only Qucusaurs can enter these one-way path formed by gaseous Phlogiston eroding rocks, and swiftly reach the exit on the other end.
: Performs a small jump. Hold consuming Phlogiston to perform a higher leap. While the Iktomisaurus is surrounded by a blue ring, the jump will be even higher.
It is a special state for Iktomisaurus and new abilities are added.
: They can absorb Phlogiston from objects, and the information and power within Nightspirit Sigils and Nightspirit Graffitis becoming able to create a corresponding Nightspirit Body.
: Place the Nightspirit Body scanned from a Nightspirit Graffiti in front of the Iktomisaurus. Hold to aim the placement. Can be used while in air too by consuming Phlogiston.
: Iktomisaurs can detect special mechanisms using a unique form of Elemental Sight.
: Throws forward a blow with its head, dealing
. Then it enter the Spirited state.
: Sprints forward and perform a Quick Charge consuming Phlogiston. When a charging Tatankasaurus hits an obstacle and is obstructed, it will enter the Spirited state.
It is a special state for Tatankasaurus and some of its abilities will change.

: The Tatankasaurus has 3 s (displayed above its head) to activate the special Dash of the Spirited state. While dashing in this state, it will consume Phlogiston to run faster and make flying leaps using terrain, push heavy objects, break through Pulverite, knock Matador Monetoo down, and even briefly dash across water or liquid Phlogiston without taking damage.
: They will be stimulated by the energy within, causing them to enter a second sprint.
: Tatankasaurs are capable of resisting the continuous lose of HP and mobility.

: With their powerful charges, they can significantly affect Oozing Cores, knock Matador Monetoo down while waving their flags and standing atop rocks, and break those dragon statue mechanism parts that fires projectiles.
: Tatankasaurus's downward stomp can cause it to collapse, causing other strange phenomena occur nearby.

They can use the grappling hook to aim in any direction and leap forward in their Nightsoul’s Blessing state. While in the air after using the ability, they can use it again to hover in the air and leap in any direction.
They can use any item Yumkasaurs can interact with in their Nightsoul’s Blessing state. While in this state, their interactions with the items will follow rules applicable to Yumkasaurs.
- In Nightsoul's Blessing State, when interacting with items Yumkasaurs can interact with, enter "Yumkasaur Mimesis" state. While in this state, Nightsoul's Blessing State will not be put on CD.
They can continuously consume Phlogiston to remain airborne.
They can enter Phlogiston Wind Tunnel and stay in Gaseous Phlogiston without being burned
- She can use Nightsoul Transmission to enter in Nightsoul's Blessing state and gain 32 Nightsoul points.
They can perform high leaps by holding the jump button and consuming Phlogiston in Natlan, or Stamina.
They can enter Spiritspeaker state by Holding Aiming Mode.
- She can use Nightsoul Transmission to perform a high leap without consuming Phlogiston and Stamina.
- He can use Nightsoul Transmission to perform a high leap without consuming Phlogiston and Stamina.
They can enter the Spirited state while in Nightsoul's Blessing state.

In this state, their Movement SPD is increased, and they can make flying leaps using terrain, push heavy objects, break through Pulverite, knock Matador Monetoo down, or consume additional Nightsoul points to move on water or liquid Phlogiston while being immune to DMG from the latter.
- She won't take fall DMG when using terrain differences to make leaps in

These mechanisms can be interacted with during the
Interact with the mechanism to swiftly reach the exit on the other end without consuming Phlogiston or Stamina.
Qucusaurs can use the Wind Currents, and won't be burned by the high temperatures of the gaseous Phlogiston.

Interacting with Gaseous Phlogiston Lumps and Phlogiston Currents will cause a state of having full Phlogiston and not consuming any. A short-lived Phlogiston Current can be created by using a Plunging Attack while in this state.
These mechanisms can be interacted with during the
Iktomisaurs detect special mechanisms using a unique form of Elemental Sight.
They can absorb the information and power from the Nightspirit Graffitis by Holding to Aim, and then create a corresponding Nightspirit Body.
This Monetoo is calmed when touched by a Wind Current created through Nightspirit Graffiti.
Iktomisaurs can leap up to high places, and enter the Spiritspeaker state by Holding to Aim, absorbing Phlogiston from objects within their field of view and further empowering their next jump.
They can Hold to Aim and extract the information within these sigils to obtain rewards or strength.
There is some special Phlogiston Engraving whose power within can be activated by Iktomisaurus Spiritspeaker state.

This cores are significantly affected by Tatankasaurus's charge immense impact.
The Tatankasaurus's strongest charge in Spirited state can knock down this mischievous Monetoo.
These dragon statue mechanism parts fires projectiles, but you can charge at the muzzle with immense power and strike a powerful blow to destroy it.
When passing through it, the Tatankasaurus will enter a second sprint in Spirited state.
Tatankasaurs can shatter these Phlogiston rocks with its attacks, and pass through them while in Spirited state.
Tatankasaurus's downward stomp can cause it to collapse and then some other strange phenomena may occur nearby.
Natlan is a nation where humans and Saurians live together.
You may have heard of this already, but seeing is believing.
The warriors in the Stadium of the Sacred Flame are about to enter the fray.
While waiting for the competition to begin,
Why not take some time to listen to our three friends share the various facets of their tribal lives?
You'll run into all kinds of Saurians during your adventures here in Natlan.
Journey alongside the Saurians and immerse yourself in the breathtaking vistas of Natlan.
Today, we'll be introducing the "Children of Echoes," one of Natlan's six tribes. Word has it that the mines near the tribes hide a lot of treasure!
Do you enjoy soaking in hot springs? If so, don't miss out on the "People of the Springs"! They have the best vacation spot, so let's learn more about them!
I've heard that people from a certain tribe really love adventure and sometimes do things that seem quite dangerous to others. Let's learn more about them!
The journey to Natlan is about to begin. Let's explore the interesting gameplay features in the new area together!
Adventure Guide | Search for the Mysterious and Legendary "Masters of the Night-Wind"...

Head west from the Stadium of the Sacred Flame and pass through the towering outer wall, there you will find a mysterious tribe — the Masters of the Night-Wind. Let us read this Adventure Guide and together remove the veil of secrecy that shrouds the Masters of the Night-Wind~
Learn to Fly With the Flower-Feather Clan!

In order to pursue the great dream in the morning mist and sea of clouds, the warriors who soared the skies never rested for a moment. Whether you're looking to explore the local culture and customs or just want to test out your flying skills, the Flower-Feather Clan is the place for you!
The Collective of Plenty Tour Group, off we go!

Welcome to the Collective of Plenty — a tribe nestled at the foot of a volcano, where strength is revered! Pack your bags and follow your temporary guide as we explore this bountiful land of farming and fecundity, and experience its unique culture and customs!
